div1.entryFree:
Clothe, to cover with dress: to c. a back, Meas. III, 2, 23. to feed and c. thee, Hml. III, 2, 64. to c. you as becomes you, Shr. IV, 2, 120. --d like a bride, Per. I, 1, 6. Followed by in: began to c. his wit in state and pride, Lucr. 1809. --ing me in these grave ornaments, H6A V, 1, 54. c. me in a forced content, Oth. III, 4, 120 (cf. Dress). in steel, Per. II, 1, 160. Followed by with: to c. mine age with angel-like perfection, Gentl. II, 4, 66. I c. my naked villany with old odd ends, R3 I, 3, 336.
Absolutely: care no more to c. and eat, Cymb. IV, 2, 266 (== to wear or provide clothes).
